g.text: * Fuel gauge. The BQ25620 does not provide this functionality,

g.desc: * Fuel gauge. The BQ25620 does not provide this functionality,

g.text: and it could be either costly (implementation-wise) or inefficient

g.desc: and it could be either costly (implementation-wise) or inefficient

g.text: (in terms of power consumption) to implement such a feature

g.desc: (in terms of power consumption) to implement such a feature

g.text: in software, using whatever data the BQ25620 provides.

g.desc: in software, using whatever data the BQ25620 provides.

g.text: [input-vbat] Battery Input: Connectors, Protections and Fuel Gauge

g.desc: [input-vbat] Battery Input: Connectors, Protections and Fuel Gauge

g.text: * This selects the battery's THRM (PPTC) destination. By default, the Fuel Gauge is

g.desc: * This selects the battery's THRM (PPTC) destination. By default, the Fuel Gauge is

g.text: selected, but when VUSB or VEXT is present, the battery charger will force itself

g.desc: selected, but when VUSB or VEXT is present, the battery charger will force itself

g.text: as the destination. ~{BC_PG} is pulled-up to 3.3V.

g.desc: as the destination. ~{BC_PG} is pulled-up to 3.3V.

g.text: * BQ25620's datasheet instructs the user to reference THRM to REGN, which is

g.desc: * BQ25620's datasheet instructs the user to reference THRM to REGN, which is

g.text: only active when VUSB is present and valid. Out of this case, temperature

g.desc: only active when VUSB is present and valid. Out of this case, temperature

g.text: information would not be available, unless read by the MAX17055, which has

g.desc: information would not be available, unless read by the MAX17055, which has

g.text: a totally different wiring scheme from the BQ. Also, MAX17055 is more

g.desc: a totally different wiring scheme from the BQ. Also, MAX17055 is more

g.text: power-efficient for this type of measurement (BQ needs the ADC to be active,

g.desc: power-efficient for this type of measurement (BQ needs the ADC to be active,

g.text: * To force THRM to go to BQ, close THRM_BYPASS, and de-populate (remove) Rthrm.

g.desc: * To force THRM to go to BQ, close THRM_BYPASS, and de-populate (remove) Rthrm.
